response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web developer with strong software craftsmanship, but his background is almost entirely web, mobile, and application-level development. The role requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture knowledge, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his experience. His low-latency DLang work and brief C/C++ stint at National Instruments show performance sensibility at the software level, but this does not translate to principal-level hardware performance architecture.
strengths: 13+ years of professional software engineering experience, exceeding the BS + 8 years bar
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (async I/O, fibers, caching at millions of requests/sec)
Some systems-level C/C++ exposure (LabWindows/CVI components, audio wave field synthesis thesis)
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon architecture, or hardware performance modeling experience whatsoever
No deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or AI accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, far from principal-level hardware-software co-design
